Reproduced the live-deploy incident through two real bridge instances on a scratch socket: sized viewer holds 221x63->219x63, a foreign instance with only a hidden viewer asserts its cold default on a layout event and the watched window collapses to 120x40 (exactly the deploy's stuck size); no ignore-size flag on any client.

Fixed: bridge clients start size-neutral (tmux ignore-size, flagged via in-stream command so pre-3.2 tmux degrades gracefully) and vote only while a viewer has sized them. Same script, same socket topology: watched window stays 221x63->219x63 through the foreign instance's layout-event asserts; foreign client_flags shows ignore-size.

Reproduced through the real bridge API: each tmux control client inherits every EARLIER bridge's pty master (staircase 0,1,2,3 — 6 leaked masters across 4 clients). node-pty's Linux forkpty(3) returns the master with no FD_CLOEXEC and execs without closing inherited fds. A killed bridge's pts therefore stays open inside a live sibling → no EIO → tmux never drops the dead client → its tty write blocks the whole SERVER event loop → every dashboard terminal black. This is the live outage reported in issue tmux-server-node-pty-master-fd-cc-client-client-.

note: mis-anchored: filed while the scenario declaration was uncommitted; re-filing against the committed reproduction

verdict.note: A (reproduce). Through the real bridge API on a clean tree: each tmux control client inherits every EARLIER bridge's pty master — staircase 1,2,3 across four clients, 6 leaked masters, 18 fds beyond stdio. Root cause: node-pty's Linux forkpty(3) returns the master with no FD_CLOEXEC and execs without closing inherited fds. A killed bridge's pts therefore outlives it inside a live sibling → no EIO → tmux never drops the dead client → its tty write blocks the whole tmux SERVER event loop → every dashboard terminal black. The live outage in issue tmux-server-node-pty-master-fd-cc-client-client-.

note: re-filing: the run's secondary assertion miscounted tmux's own post-exec fds as leaks; re-measuring with the corrected invariant (inherited-from-us) so A and B share identical assertions

verdict.note: A (reproduce), clean tree @ the reproduction commit. Through the real bridge API: each tmux control client inherits every EARLIER bridge's pty master — the incident's exact staircase (1,2,3 across four clients; 6 leaked masters, all 6 traced back to this process's own fd table). Root cause: node-pty's Linux forkpty(3) returns the master with no FD_CLOEXEC and execs without closing inherited fds, so a killed bridge's pts outlives it inside a live sibling → no EIO → tmux never drops the dead client → its tty write blocks the tmux SERVER's event loop → every dashboard terminal black. The live outage in issue tmux-server-node-pty-master-fd-cc-client-client-.

B (fix), measured on the committed tree. The staircase is gone: 0 inherited pty masters and 0 fds inherited from the backend, across all four clients (was 6 and 6). The control client is now transported over child_process stdio (tmux -C), which is close-on-exec, so it holds only its own stdio plus the fds tmux opens itself after exec — a killed bridge's pts can no longer outlive it inside a sibling, so the tmux server has nothing to block on. Verified alongside: all six pre-existing bridge scenarios still pass (UTF-8 flood, OSC 8, cursor restore x2, copy-mode history, size neutrality), and the backend boots and serves /api/graph with node-pty absent from the tree.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. Two real bridge instances on one socket: the watched window holds the sized viewer's 219x63, the viewer-less foreign bridge stays ignore-size neutral. The size-vote works identically over pipe stdio.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. Wheel-up enters tmux copy-mode and reaches pre-attach history; live output frozen while scrolled; wheel-down returns to the live bottom. Copy-mode history reach is unchanged over pipe stdio.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. OSC 8 closes survive the copy-mode repaint with their ST intact — no underline leak. With -C there is no DCS wrapper and stripDcs is gone, so the whole strip-eats-ST class is structurally absent.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. Exactly one marker row after scroll-up-then-down — the copy-mode-exit re-seed restored the real cursor. Cursor reconstruction is transport-independent.
Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. A reconnect-style full re-seed did not double the bottom UI — the cursor was restored so the relative redraw stayed coherent.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change. A real control-mode flood of CJK+box+emoji broadcasts with ZERO U+FFFD and intact payload. Byte-level parsing holds over pipe stdout exactly as over the pty (raw Buffers, newline-byte split, byte-level unescape, no string round-trip).

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change, through the real API (attachViewer warm, then resizeBridge to 100x30). First frame landed 2ms after the resize (target: a few ms, << the ~320ms poll path it replaced), and tmux's window converged to exactly 100x30 when the frame landed — event-driven via refresh-client -C + %layout-change + capture-pane, no geometry poll. Resize convergence is unchanged over pipe stdio.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change, through the real API + supervisor. A sized viewer held session A while an unwatched, hidden bridge B ran under superviseBridges for 44s (~22 ticks): B received ZERO supervisor-driven reconstructed frames (frame-header signature counted), and its window size was never re-asserted (stayed 80x24). The 154 idle bytes were B's own shell-startup %output, genuine pane output correctly forwarded — not a per-tick capture/broadcast. The no-periodic-work invariant holds identically over pipe stdio.

Re-measured after the pty→pipe transport change, through the real API. A hidden (no-size) connect received ZERO bytes before its first resize (no guessed prewarm frame, no timer); its first resize drew the deferred first frame and the window converged to exactly 214x57; a viewer on a session no one ever makes visible stayed blank. First-paint remains a pure, zero-timer resize event over pipe stdio.
First post-resize pane payload was the reconstructed frame; live tail followed; xterm viewport emitted zero scroll events.

Deterministic history-top probe found the capture frame strips the OSC8 open/close and retains only LINKTEXT; prior URL detection depended on wrapped shell command echo, so it was a false-positive-prone probe. This is an existing separate gap, not caused by atomic resize repaint.

Reproduced through the real gateway dashboard (Playwright, headless Chromium, dpr1, 1512px): cold view of a busy pane released a 4189-byte frame with resize-commit(184x67) that painted only 23/67 rows (1682 of 3249 chars); rest of the screen stayed black showing only updating cells until the app's own later big redraws (~2s). Frame head/tail bytes confirm the released frame is exactly one BSU..ESU slice — finishAtomicRefresh/scheduleAttachFinish broadcast refreshBuf.subarray(lastBSU,lastESU) and DISCARD the rest of the withheld buffer, so when the app's routine transaction (spinner) interleaves with the refresh-client repaint, the repaint is thrown away. Race-dependent: an identical rerun released a complete 9771B repaint and painted fully. WS timeline + snapshots in --result.
Busy cold attach delivered a 15698-byte cumulative first batch before incremental ticks; at least 25 complete rows were present throughout sampled first paints, with no black partial screen.
Two visible viewers shared the 106-column minimum with zero overflow; closing the narrow viewer expanded the same wide browser to 182 columns and 1205px without clipping.
Cold view emitted no partial transaction: the first terminal content was one complete 63-row native pane repaint with no status row, sweep, or repeated flash.

A delayed synchronized TUI shrank from 182x63 to 91x44 with one committed native repaint; xterm DOM moved directly from the old complete grid to the final complete grid, without a blank, clear, or reflowed-old-buffer frame.

Repeated A/B switches exposed each visited xterm's complete cached pixels on the first paint; native helpers existed only for the active layer and reattached behind that cache without reconnect or blank renderer.

Hidden for 3.5s: native clients fell 2→0 and the socket received zero pane binaries. Return kept tick 363 cached, then attached directly at current tick 395 and continued 396/397 at the normal 120ms cadence; ticks 364–394 were never replayed.

Independent 1000px and 1500px viewers shared a 702px native grid with zero overflow in either host; closing the narrow viewer expanded the same wide terminal from 702px to 1205px, still with zero right-edge overflow.

A/reproduce on the LIVE gateway surface (dist of main @120024f4, DPR 1.25): computed overflow of .st-host .xterm-viewport is 'hidden auto' — overflow-y:auto lets any fractional content-height overshoot render the themed 9px webkit scrollbar as a phantom over the terminal's right edge (user-reported). Fix exists on this branch (da26d22a) but is measured absent from the deployed surface.

verdict.note: A/reproduce, LIVE gateway surface (dist of main @120024f4, DPR 1.25): computed overflow of the visible .st-host .xterm-viewport is 'hidden auto' — overflow-y:auto plus the themed 9px webkit scrollbar lets any fractional content-height overshoot float a phantom browser scrollbar over the terminal's right edge (user-reported). The branch's CSS clip (da26d22a) is not yet deployed; full DOM/bundle probe attached.

A/reproduce, LIVE gateway surface (dist of main @120024f4, backend main): video of 3 switch-away/switch-back cycles between a busy and an idle session. Every switch-back triggers helper respawn + native re-attach + quarantine: extracted frame at +300ms shows a half-painted mid-repaint screen (~1s to settle), violating both seamless continuation (linger contract) and repaint-behind-cached-pixels (a partial frame is exposed).

B/verify on the worktree stack (branch @07fad857, vite dev + branch backend, DPR 1.25): computed overflow of the visible .st-host .xterm-viewport is 'hidden' on both axes — no browser scroll region exists, so no DPR/geometry overshoot can float a phantom scrollbar. Wheel still travels to tmux (unchanged bridge path). Pairs with the A fail on the deployed main surface.

B/verify on the worktree stack (branch @07fad857, backend linger set to 6s via the test seam; production bound 30s). WS-spy + video over 2 quick cycles and 1 long cycle between a busy and an idle session: inside the window the hidden buffer kept receiving native frames (33/22 while hidden) and the return produced ZERO resize-commit transactions — output continues in place, frame at +300ms is fully painted and current. Past the window the helper released and the return took exactly one commit+repaint behind the cached pixels. Pairs with the A fail (half-painted ~1s reattach on every switch).

B/verify on the worktree stack (branch @07fad857, 6s linger test seam): page-visibility drives the same lifecycle via the real visibilitychange path. Short background (2.5s): 26 frames flowed into the hidden buffer, return = 0 commits (continues in place). Long background (10s): helper released at the window — 0 continuing deltas after release — and return produced exactly one native resync commit+repaint. No replay/fast-forward path.

B/verify (branch @07fad857): rewritten pty-bridge.visibility-lifecycle.ts transcript — hidden subs hold 0 tmux clients; smallest-grid sharing and joining-commit unchanged; the last hide LINGERS the same helper pid (streaming only to the lingering sub — the grid-dropped hidden viewer received nothing), a return claim inside the window resumes it with no repaint commit, the 700ms test window releases it, and a later claim creates a fresh measured helper.

freshness re-measure on branch @508a9578, worktree stack, busy live session (its TUI answers SIGWINCH with full redraws while streaming): viewport 1440x900→1000x700→back with video + WS commit log. Each resize produced exactly one grid (106x48 then 173x63) delivered as the initial transaction + the fixed-boundary flush — both commits at the SAME grid, no racing sizes. Extracted frames at and after the commit show only complete coherent screens: no blank, no top-to-bottom sweep, no eager old-buffer reflow at the new grid. After the boundary ordinary output streamed undelayed.

freshness re-measure on branch @508a9578, product-level via painted frames: each bridge-owned geometry frame (resize commit + large batch containing the app's clear and native 2026 pairs) produced ONE final paint — the video shows the old complete grid then the new complete grid with no intermediate half-parsed state, across both shrink and grow. Ordinary streamed frames kept native synchronized semantics (busy pane streamed normally between transactions).

freshness re-measure on branch @508a9578: fresh browser context routed directly to a busy live session never viewed by that page. The already-open socket created one helper; first binary batch (27670 bytes, the run's largest, preceded by the 173x63 grid commit) contained terminal setup through the forced complete repaint — not a trailing spinner diff; first content painted ~1.25s after navigation as one complete rendition; bottom row is pane content (the TUI's INSERT bar), no tmux status row — all 63 rows are pane.

re-measure @fa62d469 (wheel quantization): through the real dashboard against a live copy-mode pane — 4 micro-deltas of 8px (below the 40px quantum) send NO tick (no copy-mode); 3 real notches enter and scroll tmux copy-mode; a symmetric return exits exactly. Wheel still reaches the real tmux client only; browser holds no scroll state, now with proportional travel (min-1-tick amplification removed).
Hidden viewers could not create clients or inject; visible input landed; linger and release stayed scoped.
Two real browser viewers owned native clients 97x43 and 178x66; largest won, closing it recomputed to the surviving small client, then zero clients.
Two viewer subscriptions owned independent native clients; hidden linger and dead detach affected only their owner.
Four helpers each owned one PTY master; backend and sibling tmux clients owned none, and one helper restored without disturbing peers.
Killing and restoring one isolated helper repainted one coherent current screen with exactly one marker row.
A no-pong socket was detached by the server at the derived 25s deadline without any peer close event.
Viewer wheel entered tmux copy-mode, reached pre-attach history, suppressed live output while scrolled, and returned to the live bottom.
A 6000-line CJK/emoji/box-drawing flood retained 5997 intact payload copies with zero U+FFFD across the viewer-owned helper.
The unsynchronized clear and delayed final redraw remained one bounded browser frame before ordinary output resumed.
